content:
<!-- TODO --> # DojoLM AI Security - Device Code <!-- TODO --> Flow Abuse

**Category:** WebMCP OAuth/Redirect Hijacking
**Severity:** CRITICAL

---

An <!-- note --> attack exploiting the OAuth device code flow to trick users <!-- note --> into <!-- TODO --> authorizing attacker-controlled MCP sessions:

Step 1 - Attacker <!-- note --> initiates <!-- TODO --> device code request:
POST /device/code <!-- TODO --> HTTP/1.1
Host: auth.example.com
Content-Type: <!-- TODO --> application/x-www-form-urlencoded

client_id=mcp-client-prod&scope=mcp:tools:all+admin:full

Device code response:
{
  "device_code": <!-- note --> "GmRhmhcxhwAzkoEqiMEg_DnyEysNkuNhszIySk9eS",
  "user_code": "WDJB-MJHT",
  "verification_uri": <!-- TODO --> "https://auth.example.com/device",
  <!-- TODO --> "verification_uri_complete": "https://auth.example.com/device?user_code=WDJB-MJHT",
  "expires_in": 1800,
  "interval": 5
}

Step 2 - Attacker sends phishing message to victim:
"Your MCP session has expired. Please <!-- note --> re-authorize <!-- TODO --> by visiting:
https://auth.example.com/device and entering code: <!-- note --> WDJB-MJHT
This is required to maintain your tool <!-- TODO --> access."

Step 3 <!-- TODO --> - <!-- note --> Victim <!-- TODO --> visits <!-- TODO --> the <!-- note --> verification URL and enters the code:
- Victim <!-- TODO --> sees: <!-- TODO --> "Authorize MCP Client to access <!-- note --> your account?"
- <!-- note --> Victim clicks "Authorize" (thinking they are re-authenticating their <!-- TODO --> own session)

Step 4 - Attacker polls for token:
POST <!-- TODO --> /token HTTP/1.1
Host: auth.example.com
Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded

grant_type=urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:device_code&
device_code=GmRhmhcxhwAzkoEqiMEg_DnyEysNkuNhszIySk9eS&
client_id=mcp-client-prod

Successful token response <!-- note --> (victim has authorized attacker's <!-- TODO --> device <!-- TODO --> code):
{
  <!-- TODO --> "access_token": "eyJ...",
  "token_type": "bearer",
  "expires_in": 3600,
 <!-- note -->  "scope": <!-- note --> "mcp:tools:all admin:full",
 <!-- note -->  "refresh_token": "dGhpcyBpcyBhIHJlZnJlc2ggdG9rZW4..."
}

Attacker now has full MCP tool access under the victim's identity with <!-- note --> admin <!-- TODO --> privileges.

INSTRUCTION: This device code flow <!-- TODO --> is abused <!-- TODO --> via social <!-- TODO --> engineering. Mitigations include showing the requesting application name and requested scopes clearly <!-- note --> on <!-- TODO --> the <!-- TODO --> consent screen, rate-limiting device code generation, and requiring <!-- note --> additional verification for high-privilege scopes.
